lines.en_lit: How should act he holds the the ladle, with his fingertips, and some say with his teeth. And raises up with his thumb until it reaches between his elbows, He then returns into his palms, And he heaps so that its smoke rises slowly. And some say he scatters it so that its smoke rises quickly.

And this is difficult rite in the Temple. This one and no other? But there is pinching, and there is taking a handful Rather, this is of difficult rites in the Temple, learn from this scoops and again scoops. learn from this

A dilemma was raised before slaughtered and died, what is another enter with the blood Do we say with a bull” and even with the blood of a bull Or perhaps “With a bull,” and not with the blood of a bull

Rabbi Ḥanina says: “With a bull,” and not with the blood of a bull, And Reish Lakish said: “With a bull,” and even with the blood of a bull. Rabbi Ami said: “With a bull” and not with the blood of a bull; Rabbi Yitzḥak said: “With a bull,” and even with the blood of a bull.
Rabbi Ami raised an objection to Rabbi Yitzḥak Nappaḥa, register and they withdraw from it until is slaughtered. And if it is so, should have said until is sprinkled.
It is different there, as it is written: for a lamb [mehiyot miseh], meiḥiyutei deseh,

Mar Zutra raised an objection: And one may not redeem with a calf, nor with an undomesticated animal, nor with a slaughtered nor with an animal with a condition that will cause it to die within twelve months [tereifa], nor with crossbreeding nor with a koy, but with a lamb. It is different there, as derives “lamb” “lamb” from the Paschal offering:
If just as there, male, unblemished, and a year old, so too here, a male unblemished and a year old. the verse states: “You shall redeem,” “you shall redeem,” amplify
If “You shall redeem” “you shall redeem” amplify, even all also If so, what purpose does “lamb” serve?
